  "description": "PROBLEM\nThe bead lifecycle spec (referenced as TD-031) must formally declare the distinction between reconcile-close and evidence-close to match the implementation.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:239-243 documents via code comment that 'reconcile-close is a meta-close that intentionally bypasses ClosureGate', but this distinction is not reflected in the formal specification document. The spec exists as a reference in types.go:78 (\"TD-031 §2\") but the full document is not in this repo.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nCreate or amend TD-031 (or equivalent specification document in docs/helix/03-design/) to:\n1. Define evidence-close: direct close via Store.Close, requires closing_commit_sha evidence from execution session.\n3. Document when each is used: reconcile-close for dependency satisfaction, evidence-close for work-completion evidence.\n4. Reference both concepts from the lifecycle transition documentation.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not modify the code to match spec — match spec to code. Do not move ClosureGate logic.\n",

Integration: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go passes (uses Blocked() indirectly).\n",
  "closing_commit_sha": "cb5038f726332b3239cc902ee28fa1b394384105",

  "description": "PROBLEM\nstore.go:2172 re-filters Status==Open when iterating Blocked() results, but EvaluateLifecycleQueue already classified the bucket correctly. The double-filter creates a discrepancy: the queue classification is the source of truth, but the Status check can disagree if queued beads have status != StatusOpen in the LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies bucket.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/bead/store.go:2140-2161 readyFiltered delegates to classifyLifecycleQueue for bucket classification.\ncli/internal/bead/store.go:2163-2175 Blocked() calls classifyLifecycleQueue and re-filters Status==Open at line 2172.\nlifecycle.go:EvaluateLifecycleQueue correctly identifies LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies beads regardless of Status.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Remove the Status==Open check at store.go:2172.\n2. Update Blocked() comment to clarify it returns beads in LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies; the bucket designation is the single source of truth.\n3. Verify Blocked() behavior is unchanged by running ReadyExecutionBreakdown breakdown tests.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not refactor EvaluateLifecycleQueue. Do not change the bucket classification algorithm.\n",

All sub-tests run against both jsonl and axon backends (forEachBackendConformanceCase).\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go::TestBackendConformance_TransitionMatrix passes; both backends match.\n",
  "closing_commit_sha": "0b14a5903582c2847bf40d1baba19286c7d8efa8",
  "created_at": "2026-05-18T03:36:28.435251918Z",

  "description": "PROBLEM\ncli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go currently tests create/update/claim/deps/events but does not exercise the full bead lifecycle state transition matrix defined in the spec.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe spec (TD-031, to be created in B1) defines all legal transitions, but backend_conformance_test.go only verifies basic CRUD operations. The TestBackendConformance_TransitionMatrix test exists (line 173) but is incomplete or minimal.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nExtend backend_conformance_test.go to:\n1. Document which transitions are legal (per TD-031 once available).\n2. Add sub-tests for every legal transition with both jsonl and axon backends:\n   - open -\u003e in_progress -\u003e open (claim/unclaim)\n   - open -\u003e in_progress -\u003e closed (claim then close)\n   - open -\u003e blocked (with external reason)\n   - blocked -\u003e open (unblock)\n   - open -\u003e proposed (operator required)\n   - proposed -\u003e open (operator accepted)\n   - proposed -\u003e cancelled\n   - closed -\u003e open (manual reopen)\n3. Add sub-tests for every illegal transition with both backends, verifying ValidateLifecycleTransition rejects them.\n4. Verify both backends produce identical results (conformance).\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not add stress tests or property-based tests. Do not test agent-level policies (only Store.TransitionLifecycle).\n",

  "acceptance": "1. Store.ParkToProposed(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, at time.Time) error exists in store.go.\n2. Store.ParkToProposed atomically transitions to proposed and appends an intake event.\n3. grep -r 'parkBeadPostIntakeRejection' cli/internal/agent/*.go shows 0 references (all replaced).\n4. All 6+ agent call sites (execute_bead_loop.go, execute_bead_post_review.go, etc.) use Store.ParkToProposed.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go passes.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go passes (uses intake rejection).\n",
  "created_at": "2026-05-18T03:36:36.097996051Z",
  "description": "PROBLEM\n6 call sites in cli/internal/agent/{execute_bead_loop.go, execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_review_terminal.go, execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go} duplicate the pattern of parking a bead to status=proposed with an intake event.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nparkBeadPostIntakeRejection exists in execute_bead_loop.go:4016-4050 and is called from 6+ locations, but it directly manipulates Store and appends events inline. The Store interface does not expose a single public method that encapsulates both the transition and the event atomically.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Extract a new Store.ParkToProposed(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, at time.Time) error method that:\n   - Calls TransitionLifecycle to set status=proposed with OperatorRequired=true.\n   - Appends a structured intake event.\n   - Returns any error.\n2. Update parkBeadPostIntakeRejection to use the new Store.ParkToProposed (or deprecate parkBeadPostIntakeRejection entirely if it becomes redundant).\n3. Replace all 6 agent call sites with the new Store.ParkToProposed method.\n4. Verify no functional change: output beads must have identical status and events.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change the intake rejection policy itself. Do not add new parameters or optional behavior.\n",
